content.text:
<timestamp>Wednesday, May 13, 2026, 1:58 PM (UTC+5:30)</timestamp>
<user_query>
You are a senior full-stack engineer.

Build a production-ready MVP for a Team Task Manager application.

Tech Stack:
- Frontend: React + Vite + TypeScript + TailwindCSS
- Backend: Node.js + Express + TypeScript
- Database: PostgreSQL
- ORM: Prisma
- Authentication: JWT
- Deployment: Railway

Requirements:
- Signup/Login
- Role-based access control (Admin/Member)
- Project creation
- Add project members
- Task creation
- Task assignment
- Task status updates
- Dashboard with:
  - total tasks
  - completed tasks
  - pending tasks
  - overdue tasks

Architecture Requirements:
- Clean folder structure
- Scalable architecture
- Proper error handling
- Middleware-based authentication
- Prisma schema with relationships
- REST APIs
- TypeScript everywhere
- Reusable frontend components

Generate:
1. Full folder structure
2. Backend setup
3. Frontend setup
4. Prisma schema
5. Express server setup
6. Tailwind setup
7. Environment variables
8. Railway deployment preparation

Do NOT over-engineer.
Keep the UI clean and minimal.
Focus on functionality and clean code.
</user_query>

I’ll scaffold a clean monorepo-style MVP now (frontend + backend + Prisma) and wire auth, RBAC, projects/tasks, and dashboard APIs end-to-end, then provide the final structure and setup details. I’m first checking the current workspace path and initializing the project layout.

[REDACTED]

Workspace is empty, so I’m scaffolding the entire MVP from scratch with `client` and `server` apps plus shared root scripts/config. After scaffolding, I’ll implement API/auth/database and minimal UI screens.

[REDACTED]

content.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: C:\Users\Aditya Govil\.cursor\projects\empty-window\server\prisma\schema.prisma
+generator client {
+  provider = "prisma-client-js"
+}
+
+datasource db {
+  provider = "postgresql"
+  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
+}
+
+enum Role {
+  ADMIN
+  MEMBER
+}
+
+enum TaskStatus {
+  PENDING
+  IN_PROGRESS
+  COMPLETED
+}
+
+model User {
+  id               String             @id @default(uuid())
+  name             String
+  email            String             @unique
+  passwordHash     String
+  role             Role               @default(MEMBER)
+  createdAt        DateTime           @default(now())
+  updatedAt        DateTime           @updatedAt
+  memberships      ProjectMember[]
+  assignedTasks    Task[]             @relation("AssignedTasks")
+  createdProjects  Project[]          @relation("CreatedProjects")
+  createdTasks     Task[]             @relation("CreatedTasks")
+}
+
+model Project {
+  id          String          @id @default(uuid())
+  name        String
+  description String?
+  createdById String
+  createdAt   DateTime        @default(now())
+  updatedAt   DateTime        @updatedAt
+  createdBy   User            @relation("CreatedProjects", fields: [createdById], references: [id])
+  members     ProjectMember[]
+  tasks       Task[]
+}
+
+model ProjectMember {
+  id        String   @id @default(uuid())
+  projectId String
+  userId    String
+  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
+  project   Project  @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
+  user      User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
+
+  @@unique([projectId, userId])
+}
+
+model Task {
+  id          String      @id @default(uuid())
+  title       String
+  description String?
+  dueDate     DateTime?
+  status      TaskStatus  @default(PENDING)
+  projectId   String
+  createdById String
+  assignedToId String?
+  createdAt   DateTime    @default(now())
+  updatedAt   DateTime    @updatedAt
+  project     Project     @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
+  createdBy   User        @relation("CreatedTasks", fields: [createdById], references: [id])
+  assignedTo  User?       @relation("AssignedTasks", fields: [assignedToId], references: [id])
+}
*** End Patch

content.name: ApplyPatch
content.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: C:\Users\Aditya Govil\.cursor\projects\empty-window\README.md
@@
-# Team Task Manager MVP
+# Team Task Manager MVP
+
+Production-ready MVP with:
+- React + Vite + TypeScript + Tailwind frontend
+- Express + TypeScript backend
+- Prisma + PostgreSQL
+- JWT auth + RBAC (Admin/Member)
+- Railway deployment config
+
+## 1) Folder Structure
+
+```txt
+team-task-manager-mvp/
+  client/
+    src/
+      components/
+        Card.tsx
+        ProtectedRoute.tsx
+      context/
+        AuthContext.tsx
+      pages/
+        DashboardPage.tsx
+        LoginPage.tsx
+        SignupPage.tsx
+      services/
+        api.ts
+      types/
+        index.ts
+      App.tsx
+      main.tsx
+      index.css
+    .env.example
+    index.html
+    package.json
+    postcss.config.js
+    tailwind.config.js
+    tsconfig.json
+    vite.config.ts
+  server/
+    prisma/
+      schema.prisma
+    src/
+      config/
+        env.ts
+        prisma.ts
+      controllers/
+        auth.controller.ts
+        project.controller.ts
+        task.controller.ts
+      middlewares/
+        async.middleware.ts
+        auth.middleware.ts
+        error.middleware.ts
+      routes/
+        auth.routes.ts
+        project.routes.ts
+        task.routes.ts
+      types/
+        express.d.ts
+      app.ts
+      index.ts
+    .env.example
+    package.json
+    tsconfig.json
+  .gitignore
+  nixpacks.toml
+  package.json
+  railway.json
+  README.md
+```
+
+## 2) Backend Setup
+
+```bash
+npm install
+cp server/.env.example server/.env
+```
+
+Set real values in `server/.env`, then:
+
+```bash
+npm run prisma:generate --workspace server
+npm run prisma:migrate --workspace server
+npm run dev:server
+```
+
+## 3) Frontend Setup
+
+```bash
+cp client/.env.example client/.env
+npm run dev:client
+```
+
+## 4) Prisma Schema
+
+Implemented in `server/prisma/schema.prisma`:
+- `User` (role: ADMIN/MEMBER)
+- `Project`
+- `ProjectMember` (many-to-many user↔project)
+- `Task` (status, assignee, due date)
+
+## 5) Express Server Setup
+
+- Entry point: `server/src/index.ts`
+- App config: `server/src/app.ts`
+- Middleware:
+  - JWT auth (`authenticate`)
+  - RBAC (`authorize`)
+  - async error wrapper
+  - global error handler
+
+### Core REST APIs
+
+- `POST /api/auth/signup`
+- `POST /api/auth/login`
+- `GET /api/projects`
+- `POST /api/projects` (admin only)
+- `POST /api/projects/:projectId/members` (admin only)
+- `GET /api/tasks`
+- `POST /api/tasks`
+- `PATCH /api/tasks/:taskId`
+- `GET /api/tasks/dashboard/summary`
+
+## 6) Tailwind Setup
+
+Configured in:
+- `client/tailwind.config.js`
+- `client/postcss.config.js`
+- `client/src/index.css`
+
+## 7) Environment Variables
+
+### Backend (`server/.env`)
+
+```env
+PORT=5000
+DATABASE_URL="postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/team_task_manager"
+JWT_SECRET="replace-with-strong-secret"
+CORS_ORIGIN="http://localhost:5173"
+```
+
+### Frontend (`client/.env`)
+
+```env
+VITE_API_URL="http://localhost:5000/api"
+```
+
+## 8) Railway Deployment Prep
+
+Included:
+- `railway.json` with start command
+- `nixpacks.toml` for Node 20 build/runtime
+
+### Railway steps
+
+1. Create new Railway project and connect this repo.
+2. Add a PostgreSQL service in Railway.
+3. Set backend variables:
+   - `DATABASE_URL` (from Railway Postgres)
+   - `JWT_SECRET`
+   - `CORS_ORIGIN` (frontend domain)
+   - `PORT` (Railway injects; keep optional)
+4. Deploy.
+5. For frontend deployment, host `client` separately (Vercel/Netlify/Railway static) and set `VITE_API_URL` to backend URL.
*** End Patch

ApplyPatch
ApplyPatch
ApplyPatch
ApplyPatch

content.text:
Implemented a complete MVP scaffold in your workspace at `C:\Users\Aditya Govil\.cursor\projects\empty-window` with clean, scalable structure and TypeScript across frontend/backend.

Built end-to-end:

- **Auth:** signup/login with JWT (`/api/auth/signup`, `/api/auth/login`)
- **RBAC:** `ADMIN` / `MEMBER` roles with middleware-based authorization
- **Projects:** create project, list projects, add members
- **Tasks:** create task, assign task, update task status, list tasks
- **Dashboard:** total/completed/pending/overdue task summary
- **Prisma + Postgres:** full relational schema
- **Frontend:** React + Vite + TS + Tailwind with reusable components and protected routes
- **Railway prep:** `railway.json` + `nixpacks.toml`

## Full Folder Structure

Included in `README.md` and created as actual files:

- `client/` (React + Tailwind app)
- `server/` (Express + Prisma API)
- Root deploy/config files (`package.json`, `.gitignore`, `railway.json`, `nixpacks.toml`, `README.md`)

## Key Generated Files

- **Backend setup**
  - `server/package.json`
  - `server/tsconfig.json`
  - `server/src/index.ts`
  - `server/src/app.ts`
  - `server/src/config/env.ts`
  - `server/src/config/prisma.ts`
  - `server/src/middlewares/*` (auth, async, error)
  - `server/src/controllers/*` (auth, user, project, task)
  - `server/src/routes/*` (auth, user, project, task)
- **Prisma schema**
  - `server/prisma/schema.prisma`
- **Frontend setup**
  - `client/package.json`
  - `client/vite.config.ts`
  - `client/tsconfig.json`
  - `client/src/main.tsx`
  - `client/src/App.tsx`
  - `client/src/pages/*` (`LoginPage`, `SignupPage`, `DashboardPage`)
  - `client/src/components/*` (`Card`, `ProtectedRoute`)
  - `client/src/context/AuthContext.tsx`
  - `client/src/services/api.ts`
- **Tailwind setup**
  - `client/tailwind.config.js`
  - `client/postcss.config.js`
  - `client/src/index.css`
- **Env files**
  - `server/.env.example`
  - `client/.env.example`
- **Railway deployment preparation**
  - `railway.json`
  - `nixpacks.toml`

## Notes

- I kept it functional and minimal (no over-engineering), with clear separation of concerns.
- `README.md` includes:
  - full structure
  - setup commands
  - env variables
  - API list
  - Railway deployment steps

If you want, I can now do a **second pass** to add:
- stronger project membership checks on task/project actions,
- pagination/filtering on tasks,
- and a tiny seed script for demo users/projects/tasks.

@@
 model Project {
   id          String          @id @default(uuid())
   name        String
   description String?
   createdById String
@@
   createdBy   User            @relation("CreatedProjects", fields: [createdById], references: [id])
   members     ProjectMember[]
   tasks       Task[]
+
+  @@index([createdById])
 }
@@
 model ProjectMember {
@@
   @@unique([projectId, userId])
+  @@index([userId])
 }
@@
 model Task {
@@
-  assignedToId String?
+  assignedToId String?
@@
   project     Project     @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
   createdBy   User        @relation("CreatedTasks", fields: [createdById], references: [id])
   assignedTo  User?       @relation("AssignedTasks", fields: [assignedToId], references: [id])
+
+  @@index([projectId])
+  @@index([assignedToId])
+  @@index([createdById])
+  @@index([status])
+  @@index([dueDate])

+
+    CONSTRAINT "Task_pkey" PRIMARY KEY ("id")
+);
+
+-- CreateIndex
+CREATE UNIQUE INDEX "User_email_key" ON "User"("email");
+
+-- CreateIndex
+CREATE INDEX "Project_createdById_idx" ON "Project"("createdById");
+
+-- CreateIndex
+CREATE UNIQUE INDEX "ProjectMember_projectId_userId_key" ON "ProjectMember"("projectId", "userId");
+
+-- CreateIndex
+CREATE INDEX "ProjectMember_userId_idx" ON "ProjectMember"("userId");
+
+-- CreateIndex
+CREATE INDEX "Task_projectId_idx" ON "Task"("projectId");
+
+-- CreateIndex
+CREATE INDEX "Task_assignedToId_idx" ON "Task"("assignedToId");
+
+-- CreateIndex
+CREATE INDEX "Task_createdById_idx" ON "Task"("createdById");
+
+-- CreateIndex
+CREATE INDEX "Task_status_idx" ON "Task"("status");
+
+-- CreateIndex
+CREATE INDEX "Task_dueDate_idx" ON "Task"("dueDate");
+
+-- AddForeignKey
+ALTER TABLE "Project" ADD CONSTRAINT "Project_createdById_fkey" FOREIGN KEY ("createdById") REFERENCES "User"("id") ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE;
+
+-- AddForeignKey
+ALTER TABLE "ProjectMember" ADD CONSTRAINT "ProjectMember_projectId_fkey" FOREIGN KEY ("projectId") REFERENCES "Project"("id") ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE;
+
+-- AddForeignKey
+ALTER TABLE "ProjectMember" ADD CONSTRAINT "ProjectMember_userId_fkey" FOREIGN KEY ("userId") REFERENCES "User"("id") ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE;
+
+-- AddForeignKey
+ALTER TABLE "Task" ADD CONSTRAINT "Task_projectId_fkey" FOREIGN KEY ("projectId") REFERENCES "Project"("id") ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE;
+
+-- AddForeignKey
+ALTER TABLE "Task" ADD CONSTRAINT "Task_createdById_fkey" FOREIGN KEY ("createdById") REFERENCES "User"("id") ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE;
+
+-- AddForeignKey
+ALTER TABLE "Task" ADD CONSTRAINT "Task_assignedToId_fkey" FOREIGN KEY ("assignedToId") REFERENCES "User"("id") ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE;
*** End Patch

content.text:
Here’s what changed in `client/src/pages/DashboardPage.tsx`:

- **Project name** field with placeholder `Project name`, `border-slate-200`, and Enter submits.
- **Create Project** button (replaces “Add”), disabled while the request runs or when the name is empty; shows **Creating…** while pending.
- **`POST /projects`** via the existing `api` client (auth header unchanged).
- After a successful create: clears the input, **`GET /projects`** to refresh the left list, and **`GET /dashboard`** so summary cards stay in sync.
- **Error** message if create fails (e.g. non-admin).
- Left panel lists projects with consistent borders; **empty state** when there are none.

Also aligned the page with the current backend: `load()` uses `/dashboard` and `/tasks/me`, and task status updates use **`PATCH /tasks/:id/status`**. Removed the old `/users` member UI that had no API.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Updates in `client/src/pages/DashboardPage.tsx`:

- **Form** with `onSubmit` so Enter or **Create Project** runs the same handler (`POST` via existing `api` → `/projects` → `/api/projects` with your `VITE_API_URL`).
- **Project name** field sits **above** the projects `<ul>`, with a **Project name** label and `border-slate-200` styling to match the dashboard.
- **Empty name**: submit shows **“Please enter a project name.”** (cleared when typing).
- **Server errors** still use the red line under the form.
- **Right after a successful `POST`**, the new project is merged into state so it shows **immediately**, then **`GET /projects`** replaces the list with the server order; dashboard summary is refreshed afterward.
- Tasks column controls use **`border-slate-200`** / **`text-sm`** for consistency with the projects block.

content.text:
Here’s what was wrong and what changed:

**Cause:** The project form was wrapped in `user?.role === "ADMIN"`, so it never rendered for members (or if `role` didn’t match exactly).

**Updates in `client/src/pages/DashboardPage.tsx`:**
- The **project name** field and **Create Project** button are always shown **above** the “Your projects” list (no role gate).
- Wrapped in a **slate bordered panel** (`border-slate-300`, `bg-slate-50`) so it’s easy to see.
- Submit still **`POST`**s to the projects resource via `projectsEndpoint()` → e.g. `http://localhost:5000/api/projects` when `VITE_API_URL` is `http://localhost:5000/api`.
- After a successful create, **`GET`** on the same projects URL refreshes the list (and the optimistic merge before that is unchanged).

Ensure `client/.env` has `VITE_API_URL=http://localhost:5000/api` (or your real API base including `/api`).

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Here’s what was updated:

### `client/src/components/Card.tsx`
- Cleaner layout: `rounded-xl`, light border, **hover shadow**, **hover border** tweak.
- Thin **accent bar** (slate / emerald / amber / rose) with slight hover emphasis.
- **Uppercase, tracked** labels; **larger tabular numbers** for values.
- Optional **`tone`** prop (`default` | `positive` | `caution` | `critical`); defaults to `default` so existing `<Card title value />` still works.

### `client/src/pages/DashboardPage.tsx`
- **Page shell**: `min-h-screen`, responsive **padding** (`px-4` → `lg:px-8`, `py-8` → `sm:py-10`).
- **Header**: stacks on small screens, **subtitle**, user **pill** + **log out** with `hover:bg-red-50`.
- **Summary grid**: 1 col → 2 on `sm` → 4 on `xl`, tighter gaps on mobile.
- **Panels**: shared `rounded-xl border … shadow-sm`, more **inner padding** on `sm+`.
- **Shared input / select / primary button** classes: **hover** + **focus ring** + disabled styles.
- **Project create** block: clearer hierarchy and helper copy.
- **Project rows** & **task rows**: **hover** border/background/shadow; task row **stacks on mobile**, row on `sm+`.
- **Empty states**: dashed border, centered copy.
- “Add” → **“Add task”** (same action).

### `client/src/pages/LoginPage.tsx` & `SignupPage.tsx`
- Centered **min-h-screen** layout, responsive padding, **rounded-2xl** cards.
- **Labels**, improved inputs (hover/focus), primary button **hover/active**.
- Login: **`Link`** to signup instead of a raw `<a href>`.

### `client/index.html`
- **`font-sans text-slate-900 antialiased`** on `<body>` for clearer type.

Behavior (API calls, state, validation) is unchanged aside from copy on one button label.

[REDACTED]

input.command: cd "C:\Users\Aditya Govil\.cursor\projects\empty-window" && git check-ignore -v server/.env.example client/.env.example 2>&1; git status -sb 2>&1
input.description: Verify .env.example not ignored; show git status

content.text:
Here is what was done:

### Removed (from the repo tree)
- **`mcps/`** — removed (Cursor MCP descriptors).
- **`terminals/`** — terminal log files removed (folder may be empty or recreated by Cursor while the IDE is open).
- **`agent-transcripts/`** — transcript file removed.
- **`server/.env`** — **deleted**; it contained a real Neon `DATABASE_URL` and `JWT_SECRET`. **Rotate that database password / credentials** in Neon if this URL was ever committed, pushed, or shared.
- **`client/.env`** — deleted (only had localhost API URL; safe to recreate from `client/.env.example`).

### `.gitignore` (root)
- Ignores **`node_modules/`**, **`dist/`**, **`.env` / `**/.env` / `**/.env.*`** with **`!**/.env.example`** so example env files stay committable.
- Ignores **`mcps/`**, **`terminals/`**, **`agent-transcripts/`**, **`.cursor/`**.
- Adds common noise: logs, OS junk, **`.vite/`**, coverage, **`.idea/`**, **`*.pem`**, keeps **`server/generated/`**.

### App behavior
- Copy env again from examples:  
  `cp server/.env.example server/.env` and `cp client/.env.example client/.env`, then fill in real values.

If **`server/.env` was ever pushed to GitHub**, treat it as leaked: rotate DB credentials, rotate `JWT_SECRET`, and use `git filter-repo` or BFG if you need it purged from history.

[REDACTED]
